question_structure.context: As shown in the figure, the ski jumper starts from rest at point A on the slide and, after a time $t_{0}$, flies off the platform at point O in a horizontal direction. It is known that point $O$ is the starting point of the slope, the vertical distance between point $A$ and point $O$ is $h$, the inclination angle of the slope is $\theta$, the mass of the athlete is $m$, and the acceleration due to gravity is $g$. Friction and air resistance are negligible.
question_structure.sub_question_1: The speed $v$ of the athlete when passing the platform $O$.
question_structure.sub_question_2: During the motion from point $A$ to point $O$, the average power $\overline{{P_{G}}}$ of the work done by gravity on the athlete.

sub_question_1.step_1: During the process of the athlete moving from point A to point $O$, according to the work-energy theorem, we have: $m g h={\frac{1}{2}}mv^{2}$. Solving for $v$, we obtain: $v=\sqrt{2g h}$.

sub_question_2.step_2: The average power of work done by gravity: $\overline{{P_{G}}=\frac{W}{t}=\frac{m g h}{t_{0}}$

image_captions: The image shows a person on a sled at point A, which is at a height 'h' above a horizontal plane. The sledder then travels down a curved ramp onto the horizontal plane, reaching point O at the base of the ramp. From point O, the sledder encounters a spring that is oriented on an inclined plane with an angle of θ to the horizontal. The sledder compresses the spring.

result_quantity.name: The speed of the athlete when passing the platform O
The average power of the work done by gravity on the athlete from point A to O
